title: pimelea drupacea
intro: Pimelea drupacea, also known as the raspberry pimelea or fruit-headed rice flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia. It is a shrub that produces small, white flowers and red or purple fruit. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The fruit of the pimelea drupacea plant can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are said to have various health benefits, including soothing sore throats and reducing fever.
